arts.ttl: Bayern & Real Madrid eye Man Utd's Dalot - Wednesday's gossip
arts.sum: Manchester United's Diogo Dalot might move in the summer, Viktor Gyokeres continues to attract Premier League interest and Newcastle want Ipswich's Liam Delap.

arts.ttl: Jean-Philippe Mateta to Man United transfer truth as Viktor Gyokeres decision pending
arts.sum: Manchester United have been linked with several strikers ahead of this summer; including Jean-Philippe Mateta, Viktor Gyokeres and Victor Osimhen.

arts.ttl: Arsenal to sign Nico Williams as Gyokeres makes Man Utd decision
arts.sum: Arsenal to sign Nico Williams as Gyokeres makes Man Utd decision William Saliba has confirmed his transfer plan amid significant interest from Real Madrid.

arts.ttl: Key questions answered as Man Utd reveal plans for ‘world’s greatest’ stadium
arts.sum: United are looking to move from the Old Trafford stadium they have called home since 1910.

arts.ttl: 'Special' Man Utd Star 'More Untouchable' Than Bruno Fernandes
arts.sum: Sir Jim Ratcliffe has praised Bruno Fernandes, but he's not the only untouchable player at Manchester United.

arts.ttl: Gary Neville announces Old Trafford return as Manchester United legend releases statement
arts.sum: Man United legend Gary Neville is set to return to Old Trafford this summer alongside Wayne Rooney and Paul Scholes for Soccer Aid.

arts.ttl: Man United set for summer cash windful as Bayern Munich and Real Madrid circle defender
arts.sum: Bayern Munich have joined Real Madrid in the race to sign Manchester United defender Diogo Dalot, according to reports. Outlet TeamTalk have revealed that the 25-year-old has caught the eye of Bayern boss Vincent Kompany thanks to his remarkable consistency and adaptability – while he is also admired by those at Rea Madrid. The wingback’s […] The post Man United set for summer cash windful as Bayern Munich and Real Madrid circle defender appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: Manchester United stadium plans given mixed reviews after new Old Trafford images emerge
arts.athr: newsdesk@men-news.co.uk (Thomas Fair, Manchester Evening News readers)
arts.sum: Sir Jim Ratcliffe has vowed to make it 'the world's greatest football stadium'

arts.ttl: Cost of new stadium could impact team, says Man Utd chief executive Omar Berrada
arts.sum: Berrada says the club want ‘to continue being competitive while we are building a new stadium’.

arts.ttl: Chelsea transfer news: Blues receive Alejandro Garnacho CONFIRMATION
arts.sum: The Red Devils have been tipped to consider offers for the pair amid their current financial woes, while Ruben Amorim has previously admitted that Man Utd will need to sell before they can buy this summer. Sir Jim Ratcliffe has confirmed that Manchester United will not be required to sell Alejandro Garnacho or Kobbie Mainoo during the 2025 summer transfer window.
